A look into the relationship between video attributes in and across each YouTube genre

STATS 220 Semester One, 2026

Yanz Chen

Plot 1

My first plot is a bar chart analysing the average length of YouTube videos, by genre. Podcasts turned out to be the clear winner and music the shortest by a fair margin, which were expected results for the ends of the spectrum.

Bar chart showing average YouTube video length by genre

Plot 2

My second plot is a stacked bar chart analysing the proportion of likes categories across YouTube genres. Surprisingly, besides the music category, the proportion of likes in general is quite low especially considering the number of views in general.

Stacked bar chart showing the proportion of likes categories across YouTube genres

Plot 3

My third plot is a scatter plot analysing YouTube video length throughout different periods of the day. This was an attempt to see whether or not the collection of data is affected by the time of day (the data was collected by selecting a random video recommended by YouTube). The results do not show a large effect, however it seems possible that longer podcasts and sporting videos are recommended later at night.

Scatter plot showing video length at different times of the day
